question.text: The FATCA/CRS declaration has been mandatory for all new mutual fund accounts since which date?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The FATCA (Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act) and CRS (Common Reporting Standard) self-declaration has been mandatory for all new financial accounts, including mutual fund folios, since January 2016. This requires investors to declare their tax residency status and provide relevant details for automatic exchange of financial account information between countries.
**The Trap:** While India signed the FATCA IGA (Intergovernmental Agreement) in 2015, the mandatory declaration requirement for all new accounts commenced from January 2016. The other dates are distractors that do not correspond to the FATCA/CRS mandate.
**Cross-Exam Context:** FATCA/CRS compliance is tested in NISM Series V-A and X-A. Understanding the timeline and purpose of FATCA/CRS is essential for distributors handling client onboarding.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always ensure the FATCA/CRS self-declaration form is completed at the time of account opening. Incomplete declarations will lead to account restrictions. For existing accounts, obtain the declaration during the next interaction with the client.
